triage_flags.notes: mana_cost: {1}{G} present in frontmatter; green-magic present in tags_filter.

trivia_bullets.text: Flesh Burrower is a common in Duskmourn: House of Horror (DSK, released 2024-09-27), a set designed around 1980s-style horror as a conscious departure from Innistrad's gothic literary tradition. The plane is a single infinite haunted house.
sources: magic.wizards.com: Duskmourn: House of Horror Mechanics

trivia_bullets.text: "Symptoms of a burrower bite include fevers, chills, gangrene, subcutaneous writhing, a growing urge to eat the flesh of the living, and if you're lucky, death." — written as a clinical medical list that escalates to horror; 'if you're lucky, death' inverts the medical convention of death as worst outcome.

Pauper-legal and PauperCommander-legal, making it one of the few DSK commons with competitive traction outside draft. Its attack trigger shares deathtouch with a second attacker; draft analysis identified it as a useful common in GW Survival builds where attacking is already mechanically rewarded.

EDHREC rank 9,537 as of Scryfall data pull, considerably higher than the other DSK commons in this batch, reflecting Commander community adoption of the deathtouch-spreading trigger in go-wide deathtouch strategies.
Illustrated by Maxime Minard, who has a presence on the Art of Magic the Gathering artist index. No Hipsters of the Coast artist profile was found.

related_cards.name: Basilisk Collar

related_cards.note: commander that grants deathtouch to tapped creatures; strong synergy with Flesh Burrower's attack trigger
